vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: extend the deterministic release pipeline so `task release -- <version>` (and `scripts/release.py`) accept an optional `--summary "..."` flag that injects a one-line Markdown blockquote at the top of the promoted CHANGELOG `[<version>]` section. The same blockquote flows into the GitHub release body (via the existing `_section_for_version` -> notes path) and is the canonical source for the Phase 8 Slack `*Summary*:` slot. Per-[AXIOM] (deterministic > Taskfile > vBRIEF > RFC2119 > prose) the implementation lands at the deterministic tier (script function + argparse + tests) with a thin skill-tier wrapper (`deft-directive-release` Phase 1 / 2 / 4 / 8 prose) covering the user-facing prompt + drift anti-pattern.

plan.title: Release CHANGELOG promotion accepts --summary blockquote (per [AXIOM] deterministic tier)

narratives.Problem: When cutting v0.X.Y the operator hand-writes the same one-line release narrative three separate times in three separate surfaces -- (1) the CHANGELOG `[<version>]` section (currently has no canonical place for it; the `### Added` bullet list is too granular), (2) the GitHub release body (currently picked up from the CHANGELOG section verbatim via `_section_for_version`, so inherits the gap), and (3) the Phase 8 Slack `*Summary*:` slot. Three transcriptions across three audiences is the kind of drift that the v0.21.0 re-cut prep discussion flagged: the GitHub release body and the Slack post diverged from the CHANGELOG section because nothing carried the operator's intended narrative as a single canonical artifact.
narratives.Overview:
Per the `## Rule Authority [AXIOM]` block in `main.md` the canonical encoding tier for a deterministic flow is the script + Taskfile, with prose tiers (skill / lessons) reserved for fallback. The proposed change therefore lands at: (Tier 1 primary) `scripts/release.py::promote_changelog` accepts a new `summary: str | None = None` keyword arg that, when truthy, injects two lines (`> <summary>
` + blank) directly after the `## [<version>] - <date>` heading and before the first sub-section so the promoted block reads `## [v] - d 

 > summary 

 ### Added 
 - ...`; `_section_for_version` already returns whatever sits between the heading and the next `## [` so the blockquote naturally flows into the GitHub release body without further plumbing; (Tier 1 secondary) `ReleaseConfig` gains a `summary: str | None = None` field; `_build_parser` adds `--summary TEXT` (recommended 80-160 chars; single-line; preserves inline Markdown verbatim); `run_pipeline` Step 4 threads `summary=config.summary` through `promote_changelog(...)` and reflects whether a summary was supplied in the dry-run preview message; `main` wires `args.summary` into the `ReleaseConfig` constructor; (Tier 4 secondary) `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md` Phase 1 / Phase 2 / Phase 4 prose covers the user-facing operator prompt (collect once, present in dry-run, pass through to production) and Phase 8 covers the canonical `*Summary*:` populate rule + the drift anti-pattern (operator MUST NOT manually rewrite the summary in Slack to deviate from CHANGELOG).
narratives.Background: v0.21.0 re-cut prep surfaced that the post-publish narrative is currently regenerated three times. The Phase 8 Slack template already has a `*Summary*:` slot but no upstream source -- operators were hand-writing it after the fact, so the GitHub release body (auto-pulled from the CHANGELOG section) and the Slack post diverged on every cut. The simplest deterministic fix is to add a single `--summary` flag at the top of the pipeline so the operator commits to one wording at Phase 1 and that wording propagates through Phase 4 promotion, Phase 4 release-body pickup (via `_section_for_version`), and Phase 8 Slack populate.
narratives.Constraint: MUST stay backward-compatible: `summary=None` (or empty) preserves the existing promoted-section shape byte-for-byte (the existing `TestPromoteChangelog` battery must keep passing unchanged). MUST keep summary inline-Markdown verbatim (operators may include `**bold**`, `[link](url)` etc. -- no escaping). Newlines in the input MUST either be normalised to a single space OR cause a clear `ValueError` (pick the simpler implementation; document the choice in tests). MUST keep the deterministic preview path observable: the dry-run Step-4 message MUST surface whether a summary was supplied (truncated to ~60 chars to keep operator-readable). MUST NOT introduce drift between the Phase 8 Slack `*Summary*:` and the CHANGELOG blockquote -- the SKILL anti-pattern explicitly prohibits operator hand-rewriting in Slack to a different wording.
narratives.Risk: Low. Additive at the framework level: new optional kwarg defaults to None, new optional `--summary` argparse flag, new `ReleaseConfig.summary` field defaulted to None, prose appended to the release skill's existing phases. No existing call sites change behaviour. The new tests in `tests/cli/test_release_summary.py` (NEW sibling file -- the existing `test_release.py` is already 1521 lines and would breach the AGENTS.md 1000-line MUST limit further) exercise the new path (with-summary, without-summary regression, Markdown preservation, empty string == None, newline handling, backslash-group-reference regression for the #730 P1 fix, argparse wiring, pipeline threading) so a future regression on the backward-compat contract surfaces immediately.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) `promote_changelog(text, version, repo, today, summary=None)` is backward-compatible AND emits a `> <summary>` blockquote between heading and first sub-section when summary is truthy; (2) `ReleaseConfig` carries `summary: str | None = None` defaulting to None; (3) `--summary TEXT` argparse flag with helptext citing 80-160 char recommendation, GitHub release body flow, and Slack template flow; (4) `run_pipeline` Step 4 threads summary through and reflects it in the dry-run preview; (5) `main` wires `args.summary` to `ReleaseConfig.summary`; (6) `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md` Phase 1 + Phase 2 + Phase 4 prose covers the operator-prompt + dry-run-preview + production pass-through; Phase 8 carries the canonical populate rule + the drift anti-pattern; (7) `tests/cli/test_release_summary.py` (NEW sibling file) adds 10 new tests covering each surface (9 original + 1 added during the #730 review cycle for the backslash-group-reference P1 regression); (8) `task check` and `task ci:local` pass green; (9) `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased] / Added` carries an entry referencing this scope vBRIEF.
narratives.Test:
Verification (in `tests/cli/test_release_summary.py`): (a) `test_promote_changelog_with_summary` -- promoted section contains `

> Test summary
` between heading and `### Added`; (b) `test_promote_changelog_without_summary` -- regression: backward-compat unchanged when summary omitted; (c) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_markdown` -- `**bold**` / `[link](url)` preserved verbatim; (d) `test_promote_changelog_summary_empty_string` -- empty string behaves as None; (e) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_newline` -- raises ValueError on 
 / ; (f) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_backslash_group_reference` -- pins the #730 Greptile P1 fix (lambda repl in re.subn so summaries containing \1 / \g<name> survive verbatim); (g) `test_main_summary_argparse` -- `--summary` lands in `ReleaseConfig.summary`; (h) `test_default_summary_is_none` -- default value contract; (i) `test_pipeline_summary_threaded` -- dry-run preview reflects the summary; (j) `test_pipeline_no_summary_threaded` -- dry-run announces `no summary` when omitted; (k) `task check` + `task ci:local` green.
narratives.Action: Single review-cycle leaf: touches `scripts/release.py`, `tests/cli/test_release_summary.py` (NEW), `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md`, `scm/changelog.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, plus this scope vBRIEF. No new GitHub issue at this time -- PR uses `Refs <discussion>` per parent's instructions; user may file a retroactive issue at their discretion.

items.title: promote_changelog accepts optional summary; emits Markdown blockquote line between heading and first sub-section

narrative.Action: Extend `scripts/release.py::promote_changelog` signature to `(text, version, repo, today, summary=None)`. When summary is None / empty, behavior is byte-identical to the existing implementation. When summary is non-empty, inject two lines (`> <summary>` + blank line) directly after the `## [<version>] - <date>` heading and before the first sub-section so the rendered block sandwiches the blockquote with blank lines for proper Keep-a-Changelog rendering. Preserve summary verbatim (no Markdown escaping). Treat newline-in-input as a clear `ValueError` (simpler than normalisation per the parent prompt's escape hatch).
narrative.Acceptance: All seven new tests in `tests/cli/test_release.py` pass; existing `TestPromoteChangelog` battery passes unchanged.

items.title: ReleaseConfig.summary field; --summary argparse flag; pipeline thread + main wiring

narrative.Action: Add `summary: str | None = None` to `ReleaseConfig` dataclass. Add `--summary TEXT` to `_build_parser` with metavar="TEXT", default=None, helptext citing 80-160 char recommendation + GitHub release body flow + Phase 8 Slack template flow. `run_pipeline` Step 4 passes `summary=config.summary` to `promote_changelog(...)` and the dry-run preview message reflects whether a summary was supplied (truncated to ~60 chars). `main` wires `args.summary` into the `ReleaseConfig` constructor.
narrative.Acceptance: argparse + pipeline + main tests pass; dry-run preview message surfaces summary state for operator validation.

narrative.Action: Phase 1 pre-flight: add a step asking the operator for the one-line summary (recommended 80-160 chars; can be skipped). Note that the summary will appear in: (1) `CHANGELOG.md [<version>]` blockquote; (2) GitHub release body (via existing `_section_for_version` pickup); (3) Phase 8 Slack `*Summary*:` slot. Phase 2 dry-run: when invoking `task release -- <version> --dry-run --skip-tag --skip-release`, also pass `--summary "<text>"` if Phase 1 collected one; the dry-run preview should show the operator the rendered blockquote (verifies the wording). Phase 4 production draft: pass `--summary "<text>"` to `task release -- <version>`. Phase 8 Slack: add a Populate rule stating the canonical `*Summary*:` is populated VERBATIM from the CHANGELOG `[<version>]` blockquote. Add a `\u2297` anti-pattern: operator MUST NOT manually rewrite the summary in Slack to deviate from CHANGELOG (creates documentation drift across audiences).
narrative.Acceptance: Phases 1, 2, 4, 8 carry the new prose; the drift anti-pattern is added to Phase 8's anti-patterns block; existing prose unchanged.

items.title: Add tests in tests/cli/test_release_summary.py (NEW sibling file)

narrative.Action: Add to NEW file `tests/cli/test_release_summary.py` (the existing `test_release.py` is already 1521 lines -- pre-existing AGENTS.md 1000-line MUST violation; the codebase pattern is to use a sibling file, established by `test_release_skip_flags.py` for #720). Tests (mirroring existing `TestPromoteChangelog` patterns): (1) `test_promote_changelog_with_summary`; (2) `test_promote_changelog_without_summary`; (3) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_markdown`; (4) `test_promote_changelog_summary_empty_string`; (5) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_newline` (raises ValueError); (6) `test_promote_changelog_summary_with_backslash_group_reference` (added during #730 review cycle to pin the lambda-repl P1 fix); (7) `test_main_summary_argparse`; (8) `test_default_summary_is_none`; (9) `test_pipeline_summary_threaded`; (10) `test_pipeline_no_summary_threaded`.
narrative.Acceptance: All 10 tests pass under `task check`; existing 241 release tests do not regress; the new file (~390 lines) is well under the 1000-line MUST limit; the architecture mirrors the established `test_release_skip_flags.py` sibling pattern.

items.title: CHANGELOG [Unreleased]/Added entry for the new feature

narrative.Action: Add a substantial entry under `## [Unreleased] / ### Added` summarising the deterministic-tier change (script + argparse + skill phases + tests) and the three-surface flow (CHANGELOG / GitHub release / Slack `*Summary*:`). End with `Refs release-narrative-gap` rather than `Closes #N` since no issue was filed yet.
narrative.Acceptance: CHANGELOG.md `## [Unreleased] / ### Added` carries the new entry; existing entries unchanged.

Issue #716: release safety hardening (default-draft + Phase 8 template surface this feature integrates with)
